questions.text: Who was Luqman according to Islamic tradition?
questions.answer: A wise man (Hakim) who was given wisdom by Allah, though scholars differ on whether he was a Prophet (most say he was a righteous servant).

questions.text: What is the first piece of advice Luqman gave to his son?
questions.answer: 'O my son, do not associate [anything] with Allah. Indeed, association [with Him] is great injustice (Shirk)'.

questions.text: How does the Quran describe the pain of a mother in this Surah?
questions.answer: She carried the child in 'weakness upon weakness' (Wahn) and his weaning is in two years.

questions.text: What is the 'limit' of obedience to parents mentioned in Verse 15?
questions.answer: If they strive to make you associate partners with Allah of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them, but accompany them in this world with kindness.

questions.text: What was Luqman's advice regarding the scale of Allah's knowledge (the mustard seed)?
questions.answer: If a deed is the weight of a mustard seed inside a rock or in the heavens or earth, Allah will bring it forth.

questions.text: What are the four commands Luqman gave his son in Verse 17?
questions.answer: Establish prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over what befalls you.

questions.text: What social etiquette did Luqman teach regarding walking and speaking?
questions.answer: Do not turn your cheek away from people in arrogance, do not walk insolently, be moderate in your pace, and lower your voice.

questions.text: Which sound is described as the 'most disagreeable of sounds'?
questions.answer: The voice of donkeys.

questions.text: What is 'Lahwal-Hadith' (idle talk) mentioned in Verse 6?
questions.answer: Distracting speech, music, or stories used to mislead people from the path of Allah without knowledge.

questions.text: If all the trees on earth were pens and the sea supplemented by seven more seas were ink, would they exhaust the words of Allah?
questions.answer: No, the words of Allah would not be exhausted.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the creation and resurrection of all of humanity in Verse 28?
questions.answer: It is but as a single soul (it is effortless for Him).

questions.text: What happens to the 'firmest handhold' (Al-Urwatul Wuthqa)?
questions.answer: Whoever submits his face to Allah while he is a doer of good has grasped the firmest handhold.

questions.text: What are the 'five things' whose knowledge belongs only to Allah (Verse 34)?
questions.answer: The Hour, the falling of rain, what is in the wombs, what a soul will earn tomorrow, and in what land a soul will die.

questions.text: Does any soul know what it will earn tomorrow?
questions.answer: No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow.

questions.text: Does any soul know in which land it will die?
questions.answer: No soul knows in what land it will die.

questions.text: What is the status of the 'Muhsin' (doer of good) in this Surah?
questions.answer: He has grasped the firmest handhold.

questions.text: What is the 'great injustice' according to Luqman?
questions.answer: Shirk (associating partners with Allah).

questions.text: How does Allah describe the heavens in Verse 10?
questions.answer: He created them without pillars that you can see.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'firm mountains' in Verse 10?
questions.answer: To prevent the earth from shaking with the people.

questions.text: How is the 'Ar-Rahman' described in the context of the Five Keys of the Unseen?
questions.answer: As Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Acquainted.
